translation: From Šalim-Aššur, Imdī-ilum, and Pūšu-kēn and Ilī-bāni to Kuziza:

translation: 6 shekels were lost in the washing process.

translation: Your silver was deducted 16 1/2 shekels both deductions and losses.

translation: Also, according to your instructions, in addition to the 18 minas silver which Ilī-bāni brought, we acquired for you 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels refined silver at a 1 shekel (per mina per month) rate and they sealed our tablet.
translation_clean: Also, according to your instructions, in addition to the 18 minas silver which Ilī-bāni brought, we acquired for you 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels refined silver at a 1 shekel (per mina per month) rate and they sealed our tablet.

translation: 31 minas refined silver, your debt, we weighed out to Pūšu-kēn.
translation_clean: 31 minas refined silver, your debt, we weighed out to Pūšu-kēn.

translation: We asked him about your tablet and he said, "Their terms are two weeks past due. Have them pay the interest so that I may give you (pl.) his tablet. I hold your tablet for interest."
translation_clean: We asked him about your tablet and he said, "Their terms are two weeks past due. Have them pay the interest so that I may give you (pl.) his tablet. I hold your tablet for interest."

translation: Let Kuzizīya come and we will negotiate about the interest.

translation: Take care to send the 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels silver which we took for interest.
translation_clean: Take care to send the 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels silver which we took for interest.

translation: As for myself and Šumi-abiya, on the day that I heard your letter, the man's son paid the certified agent(s) of the man and I said, "Your father is my agent."

translation: As for me, copper and silver as much as their are, 8 talents of fine copper, 4 minas and 45 shekels of silver, we seized and we sealed them with seal of Šumi-abiya and my seal and deposited them in my inn.
translation_clean: As for me, copper and silver as much as their are, 8 talents of fine copper, 4 minas and 45 shekels of silver, we seized and we sealed them with seal of Šumi-abiya and my seal and deposited them in my inn.

translation: They sent it for its release.

translation: Now, they will go and we will seize (the goods).

translation: 4 talents of fine copper is deposited in my inn at Ulama.

translation: Now, we will write to them and we will seize him.
translation_clean: Now, we will write to them and we will seize him.

translation_clean: He will pay 13 ḫamuštum-weeks from the ḫamuštum-week of Šū-Kūbum and Kurub-Ištar at the festival of Parka which is coming.

translation: If he does not pay when the term is full, he will add 2 1/2 shekels per mina.

translation: Now, their sealed tablet they bring to you.
translation_clean: Now, their sealed tablet they bring to you.

translation_clean: My dear brother, you and Innāya take half of the tin and textiles in Atata's charge and in accordance with (what we discussed) here, send the 'corresponding' silver.

translation: She is satisfied, she will not come back on it.

translation_clean: Seal of Ali-ahum, son of Zumaya

translation_clean: Aššur-ṣulūlī, son of Aššur-šād-ilē, who represents Kuliya, son of Ali-abum, and Aššur-emūqī, son of Šu-rama, in Kanesh, by mutual agreement seized us (as witnesses) and Aššur-ṣulūlī said to Aššur-emūqī: Your brother Bēlum-bāni owes a surety deposit to Kuliya and in his contract it is marked as (due to) 'the creditor'.

translation_clean: If you have made an agreement with Bēlum-bāni's creditor(s) and have taken silver, or have taken anything wherever silver of Bēlum-bāni is avaialbe, you shall pay all the silver he owes to Kuliya.

translation: We have settled accounts and we paid 25 minas and 4 1/4 shekels (of silver) as dātum.
translation_clean: We have settled accounts and we paid 25 minas and 4 1/4 shekels (of silver) as dātum.

translation: Iddin-Aššur is liable for the settlement of accounts; since five years we keep sending messages to him to Wahšušana and Durhumit, but until this very day he holds us in play.
translation_clean: Iddin-Aššur is liable for the settlement of accounts; since five years we keep sending messages to him to Wahšušana and Durhumit, but until this very day he holds us in play.

translation: Make him pay the silver over there his dātum for a full share.
translation_clean: Make him pay the silver over there his dātum for a full share.

translation: If he objects and says: 'I cannot match the full amount', make him pay for half the amount.
translation_clean: If he objects and says: 'I cannot match the full amount', make him pay for half the amount.

translation: If he says: 'For the accounting I can match neither the full amount nor half of it', make him swear by Aššur's dagger that he has not enough for 40 minas of silver, that 30 minas of silver is too much for him, that he does not even have 20 minas of silver, half his due, that neither silver of himself, nor of his investors in the City nor of those who live in Anatolia, <that> with whatever I carry with me or have left behind or have send ahead, that for the accounting, I cannot match either a full share or a half-share.
translation_clean: If he says: 'For the accounting I can match neither the full amount nor half of it', make him swear by Aššur's dagger that he has not enough for 40 minas of silver, that 30 minas of silver is too much for him, that he does not even have 20 minas of silver, half his due, that neither silver of himself, nor of his investors in the City nor of those who live in Anatolia, that with whatever I carry with me or have left behind or have send ahead, that for the accounting, I cannot match either a full share or a half-share.

translation: Then make him swear by the dagger and let him state before the dagger his šaddu'utu-tax, and that he does not owe any additional šaddu'utu-taxes apart from the one he states under oath.
translation_clean: Then make him swear by the dagger and let him state before the dagger his šaddu'utu-tax, and that he does not owe any additional šaddu'utu-taxes apart from the one he states under oath.

translation: Take the šaddu-utum he owes.
translation_clean: Take the šaddu-utum he owes.

translation: Of the 2 minas and 15 shekels of silver which, because of the payment for the house, Bēlum-bāni, Ennam-Suen and Peruwa, had obtained from Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, and for which their valid deed had been drawn up, 5/6 mina thereof Bēlum-bāni paid with my silver.
translation_clean: Of the 2 minas and 15 shekels of silver which, because of the payment for the house, Bēlum-bāni, Ennam-Suen and Peruwa, had obtained from Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, and for which their valid deed had been drawn up, 5/6 mina thereof Bēlum-bāni paid with my silver.

translation: For 25 shekels of silver I kept a debt-note and a lot of interest has accrued and he has agreed with me on an amount of 5/6 mina of silver.
translation_clean: For 25 shekels of silver I kept a debt-note and a lot of interest has accrued and he has agreed with me on an amount of 5/6 mina of silver.

translation: 1/2 mina of spoiled silver has been added for me.
translation_clean: 1/2 mina of spoiled silver has been added for me.

translation: 21 shekels of silver, the price of three kutānu-textiles of current quality that he deposited on Bēlum-bāni's account in the kārum-office - in all 1 2/3 mina and 1 shekel of silver.
translation_clean: 21 shekels of silver, the price of three kutānu-textiles of current quality that he deposited on Bēlum-bāni's account in the kārum-office - in all 1 2/3 mina and 1 shekel of silver.

translation: Separately, as for the 1 mina of silver, which on behalf of Anah-ilī, the son of Ṭākī, I guaranteed to Aššur-ṭāb, son of Abu-šalim, Aššur-mālik brought me a letter of Aššur-ṭāb, saying: At the accounting Aššur-ṭāb has transferred that silver (claim) to me.
translation_clean: Separately, as for the 1 mina of silver, which on behalf of Anah-ilī, the son of Ṭākī, I guaranteed to Aššur-ṭāb, son of Abu-šalim, Aššur-mālik brought me a letter of Aššur-ṭāb, saying: At the accounting Aššur-ṭāb has transferred that silver (claim) to me.

translation: All together Aššur-mālik has charged me 2 2/3 minas and 1 shekel of silver.
translation_clean: All together Aššur-mālik has charged me 2 2/3 minas and 1 shekel of silver.

translation: In the presence of Iddin-Suen, son of Kukulānum, of Šu-Išhara, son of Kurara, of Aššur-ṣululī, son of Ennam-Suen, of Aššur-bāni, the scribe.
translation_clean: In the presence of Iddin-Suen, son of Kukulānum, of Šu-Išhara, son of Kurara, of Aššur-ṣululī, son of Ennam-Suen, of Aššur-bāni, the scribe.

translation: I had been ordered to make this silver arrive in the City, but after Aššur-mālik had died, I paid 2 2/3 minas of 1 shekel of silver in Kanesh to šu-Išhara, son of Kurara, Kurarā, son of Aššur-ālik, and to Labān-nādā, the attorney, in the presence Amur-Šamaš, son of Puzur-ilī, of Aššur-bāni, son of the priest of Ea-šarrum, of Aššur-imittī, son of GADUbatum, of Ennam-Aššur, son of Ennānum, son of Kuprurak, (and) of Aššur-bāni, the scribe.
translation_clean: I had been ordered to make this silver arrive in the City, but after Aššur-mālik had died, I paid 2 2/3 minas of 1 shekel of silver in Kanesh to šu-Išhara, son of Kurara, Kurarā, son of Aššur-ālik, and to Labān-nādā, the attorney, in the presence Amur-Šamaš, son of Puzur-ilī, of Aššur-bāni, son of the priest of Ea-šarrum, of Aššur-imittī, son of GADUbatum, of Ennam-Aššur, son of Ennānum, son of Kuprurak, (and) of Aššur-bāni, the scribe.

translation: The letter addressed to Aššur-ṭāb concerning the 1 mina of silver they gave me (and) I have a letter of Aššur-ṭāb in my possession.
translation_clean: The letter addressed to Aššur-ṭāb concerning the 1 mina of silver they gave me (and) I have a letter of Aššur-ṭāb in my possession.

translation: To Iddin-Kūbum, Anah-ilī, and Puzur-Aššur, especially to Anah-ilī, thus says Kuliya:
translation_clean: To Iddin-Kūbum, Anah-ilī, and Puzur-Aššur, especially to Anah-ilī, thus says Kuliya:

translation: You owe to Abu-šalim, son of Ennam-Suen, 1 1/3 mina of silver plus its accumulated interest.
translation_clean: You owe to Abu-šalim, son of Ennam-Suen, 1 1/3 mina of silver plus its accumulated interest.

translation: A letter of Aššur-ṭab arived here and for an amount of 1 mina of silver your affair has been settled.
translation_clean: A letter of Aššur-ṭab arived here and for an amount of 1 mina of silver your affair has been settled.

translation: I myself have guaranteed that amount of silver.
translation_clean: I myself have guaranteed that amount of silver.

translation: Moreover, you have assigned 1/3 silver to our father [in the Gates of the god].
translation_clean: Moreover, you have assigned 1/3 silver to our father [in the Gates of the god].

translation: I drew up your tablet for a total of 1 1/3 mina of silver; you have in your possession a letter with my seal (good) for 1 mina of silver and a second (good) for 1/3 mina of silver.
translation_clean: I drew up your tablet for a total of 1 1/3 mina of silver; you have in your possession a letter with my seal (good) for 1 mina of silver and a second (good) for 1/3 mina of silver.

translation: You told me as follows: 'Go and obtain for me a tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab,' and I obtained for you a tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab, stating that neither his father nor he himself will come back on you and your sons.
translation_clean: You told me as follows: 'Go and obtain for me a tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab,' and I obtained for you a tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab, stating that neither his father nor he himself will come back on you and your sons.

translation: Please, my brother, put 1 1/3 mina of silver under seal and entrust it to Puzur-Aššur to bring it here.
translation_clean: Please, my brother, put 1 1/3 mina of silver under seal and entrust it to Puzur-Aššur to bring it here.

translation: Send me a message then I will give both the tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab and the one of 1 1/3 mina of silver with your own seal to your representatives.
translation_clean: Send me a message then I will give both the tablet sealed by Aššur-ṭab and the one of 1 1/3 mina of silver with your own seal to your representatives.

translation: And then they should release to me, the two letters of mine with my seal, which you have in possession, to cancel them.
translation_clean: And then they should release to me, the two letters of mine with my seal, which you have in possession, to cancel them.

translation: My dear brother, it is now two years that you keep this silver at your disposal.
translation_clean: My dear brother, it is now two years that you keep this silver at your disposal.

translation: Though I sent my servants to you, you did not send me the silver and this caused me expenses of six shekels of silver.
translation_clean: Though I sent my servants to you, you did not send me the silver and this caused me expenses of six shekels of silver.

translation: Please, my brother, send the silver and give [ the six shekels of silver, the expenses he incurred, to Puzur-Aššur.
translation_clean: Please, my brother, send the silver and give [ the six shekels of silver, the expenses he incurred, to Puzur-Aššur.

translation: Realize what my favors to you mean!
translation_clean: Realize what my favors to you mean!

translation: The judges passed the following verdict:
translation_clean: The judges passed the following verdict:

translation: The 6 shekels of silver that Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum, had given to Būṣi, wife of Iddin-Adad, and for which for the eponymy of Aššur-imittī, the boatman, she would add an interest of 1/2 mina per mina per year - for this silver and the accumulated interest on it Kapsiya, the wife of Ali-abum will sue Bṣi, wife of Iddin-Adad.
translation_clean: The 6 shekels of silver that Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum, had given to Būṣi, wife of Iddin-Adad, and for which for the eponymy of Aššur-imittī, the boatman, she would add an interest of 1/2 mina per mina per year - for this silver and the accumulated interest on it Kapsiya, the wife of Ali-abum will sue Bṣi, wife of Iddin-Adad.

translation: Akiya, son of Puzur-Aššur, Šamaš-bāni, son of Ennam-Suen, (and) Mannum-kī-Aššur, son of Ahu-waqar - these gentlemen were the judges.
translation_clean: Akiya, son of Puzur-Aššur, Šamaš-bāni, son of Ennam-Suen, (and) Mannum-kī-Aššur, son of Ahu-waqar - these gentlemen were the judges.

translation: To Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:
translation_clean: To Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:

translation: We are well, your youngsters are well, and your people are well.
translation_clean: We are well, your youngsters are well, and your people are well.

translation: Like/Instead of your .... of your father ..... your words .... on me (?).....
translation_clean: Like/Instead of your .... of your father ..... your words .... on me (?).....

translation: Who could give me ..... ........
translation_clean: Who could give me ..... ........

translation: By what you .... you have treated me .... ....
translation_clean: By what you .... you have treated me .... ....

translation: ... do not .... since no letter of yours arrives here.
translation_clean: ... do not .... since no letter of yours arrives here.

translation: What is the purpose of constantly writing me as follows: Send me five textiles!
translation_clean: What is the purpose of constantly writing me as follows: Send me five textiles!

translation: What should your boy eat, what should I (use to) pay for your expenditures if I had not humbled myself for your father.
translation_clean: What should your boy eat, what should I (use to) pay for your expenditures if I had not humbled myself for your father.

translation: He said: He is destroying you .... he has evil intentions!
translation_clean: He said: He is destroying you .... he has evil intentions!

translation: Your daughter is/keeps .... with me she lives .... of our paternal house .... [.........
translation_clean: Your daughter is/keeps .... with me she lives .... of our paternal house .... [.........

translation: I have sold the savings and slaughtered the ox.
translation_clean: I have sold the savings and slaughtered the ox.

translation: The meat which remained they have stored below.
translation_clean: The meat which remained they have stored below.

translation: I have spent 4 talents of copper (for) your youngsters must not die!
translation_clean: I have spent 4 talents of copper (for) your youngsters must not die!

translation: 1 1/3 mina and 2 shekels of silver on behalf of Kuliya, son of Ali-abum, I paid to Aššur-mālik son of Kurara.
translation_clean: 1 1/3 mina and 2 shekels of silver on behalf of Kuliya, son of Ali-abum, I paid to Aššur-mālik son of Kurara.

translation: The golden gods of Ilī-pī-uṣur and an encased tablet of 1 2/3 mina of silver, being the debt of Kuliya, Amur-šamaš and Ennam-Aššur I hold as pledge.
translation_clean: The golden gods of Ilī-pī-uṣur and an encased tablet of 1 2/3 mina of silver, being the debt of Kuliya, Amur-šamaš and Ennam-Aššur I hold as pledge.

translation: When Ana .......'s son and Ilī-pī-uṣur's attorney come here they will pay me the silver and I will release the gods.
translation_clean: When Ana .......'s son and Ilī-pī-uṣur's attorney come here they will pay me the silver and I will release the gods.

translation: As for the tablet, it will be cancelled.
translation_clean: As for the tablet, it will be cancelled.

translation: Should they not pay the silver, then Kuliya will pay me the silver and the interest on it wherever we meet.
translation_clean: Should they not pay the silver, then Kuliya will pay me the silver and the interest on it wherever we meet.

translation: Month IX, eponymy of Aššur-rē'ī, son of Ilī-emūqī.
translation_clean: Month IX, eponymy of Aššur-rē'ī, son of Ilī-emūqī.

translation: When I paid the silver Ennam-Aššur and Amur-Šamaš, son of Ilī-pī-uṣur, acted as bērū.
translation_clean: When I paid the silver Ennam-Aššur and Amur-Šamaš, son of Ilī-pī-uṣur, acted as bērū.

translation: In the presence of Aššur-malik, of Sukkalia, of Mannum-bālum-Aššur.
translation_clean: In the presence of Aššur-malik, of Sukkalia, of Mannum-bālum-Aššur.

translation: Say to Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:
translation_clean: Say to Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:

translation: Here, as for the 1 1/3 mina of silver which we borrowed to supplement the amount of Sabasiya's house/firm, my guarantors, which assisted me, all five of them, are complaining.
translation_clean: Here, as for the 1 1/3 mina of silver which we borrowed to supplement the amount of Sabasiya's house/firm, my guarantors, which assisted me, all five of them, are complaining.

translation: They say: 'Give us a tablet with your seal to the effect that the silver has been paid for Kuliya's expenses; we have borrowed the silver at an interest of 7 shekels per mina'.
translation_clean: They say: 'Give us a tablet with your seal to the effect that the silver has been paid for Kuliya's expenses; we have borrowed the silver at an interest of 7 shekels per mina'.

translation: Please, provide your father with 5 shekels of silver and each of your folks with one shekel of silver and let me no longer hear their complaints!
translation_clean: Please, provide your father with 5 shekels of silver and each of your folks with one shekel of silver and let me no longer hear their complaints!

translation: Please, act as the gentleman you are!
translation_clean: Please, act as the gentleman you are!

translation: Please, give silver to Aššur-ṣululī to bring it to me.
translation_clean: Please, give silver to Aššur-ṣululī to bring it to me.

translation: Here they are frightening me.
translation_clean: Here they are frightening me.

translation: Thus says Aššur-šad-ilē to Kuliya and Aššur-lam(assi) and in particular to Kuliya:
translation_clean: Thus says Aššur-šad-ilē to Kuliya and Aššur-lam(assi) and in particular to Kuliya:

translation: Because of the affair of Sabasiya's house the case that was not settled in your presence, Latarak has seized your merchandise that you had left behind in Apum.
translation_clean: Because of the affair of Sabasiya's house the case that was not settled in your presence, Latarak has seized your merchandise that you had left behind in Apum.

translation: When I heard it I availed myself of a tablet of the Elders and a messager who went to Apum and obtained the release of the merchandise and Abī-wēdum is now leading that to you.
translation_clean: When I heard it I availed myself of a tablet of the Elders and a messager who went to Apum and obtained the release of the merchandise and Abī-wēdum is now leading that to you.

translation: As for your house, Uṣurānum refuses to give either copper or gold saying: 'All has been consumed'.
translation_clean: As for your house, Uṣurānum refuses to give either copper or gold saying: 'All has been consumed'.

translation: From a merchant we borrowed beer bread for the banquet and for drinking flasks and we gave them to Ilī-bāni's wife.
translation_clean: From a merchant we borrowed beer bread for the banquet and for drinking flasks and we gave them to Ilī-bāni's wife.

translation: There is absolutely no reason to get nervous.
translation_clean: There is absolutely no reason to get nervous.

translation: Gentleman as you are, convert your merchandise into silver and come immediately here.
translation_clean: Gentleman as you are, convert your merchandise into silver and come immediately here.

translation: Me dear brother realizes that every single shekel of silver at hand [will be used?] for the banquet.
translation_clean: Me dear brother realizes that every single shekel of silver at hand [will be used?] for the banquet.

translation: Tell Aššur-lamassi: 'Send the silver ( and so ) encourage us'!
translation_clean: Tell Aššur-lamassi: 'Send the silver ( and so ) encourage us'!

translation: Let him give the yield of his textiles of good quality to Aššur-ṣululī.
translation_clean: Let him give the yield of his textiles of good quality to Aššur-ṣululī.

translation: Do not make him angry.
translation_clean: Do not make him angry.

translation: Thus say Aššur-šad-ilē and Ababaya, say to Kuliya:
translation_clean: Thus say Aššur-šad-ilē and Ababaya, say to Kuliya:

translation: As for your tablet which Šu-Suen, son of Innāya, has in his possession, when here their affair was concluded by (decision of) the man who settled his case they would release your tablet to us and then we would release the tablet of the City of/concerning Aššur rēşī.
translation_clean: As for your tablet which Šu-Suen, son of Innāya, has in his possession, when here their affair was concluded by (decision of) the man who settled his case they would release your tablet to us and then we would release the tablet of the City of/concerning Aššur rēşī.

translation: When we kept asking Šu-Suen for the tablet he said: 'I will give it you', by the man has left without us knowing it!
translation_clean: When we kept asking Šu-Suen for the tablet he said: 'I will give it you', by the man has left without us knowing it!

translation: If over there he asks you for silver, do not give it.
translation_clean: If over there he asks you for silver, do not give it.

translation: Tell him: 'By decision of the man who settled the affair you have been ordered to hand over in the City the tablet you have.
translation_clean: Tell him: 'By decision of the man who settled the affair you have been ordered to hand over in the City the tablet you have.

translation: And we here ............. we will ask [the man] who settled the affair and the .......... which after Abu-Šalim [had left] we had obtained ...... from/with Puzur-[....], send us that.
translation_clean: And we here ............. we will ask [the man] who settled the affair and the .......... which after Abu-Šalim [had left] we had obtained ...... from/with Puzur-[....], send us that.

translation: Send Aš[šur-ṣululī here, send (also) ...... oil'.
translation_clean: Send Aš[šur-ṣululī here, send (also) ...... oil'.

translation: 1 1/3 mina of silver from the silver of Abu-šalim, son of Ennam-Suen, which Hut...... gave to Anah-ilī, son of Ṭāb-ṣilli-Aššur, and for which he drew up a valid record and in which tablet, sealed by Anah-ilī, either the name of Abu-šalim, or simply "the creditor" is indicated - with the silver and the interest on it of this tablet Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, Kuliya, son of Ali-abum, and Šu-Bēlum, son of Puqidi, the representatives of Aššur-ṭāb, have been satisfied.
translation_clean: 1 1/3 mina of silver from the silver of Abu-šalim, son of Ennam-Suen, which Hut...... gave to Anah-ilī, son of Ṭāb-ṣilli-Aššur, and for which he drew up a valid record and in which tablet, sealed by Anah-ilī, either the name of Abu-šalim, or simply "the creditor" is indicated - with the silver and the interest on it of this tablet Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, Kuliya, son of Ali-abum, and Šu-Bēlum, son of Puqidi, the representatives of Aššur-ṭāb, have been satisfied.

translation: The tablet of the debt of Anah-ilī [that may turn up] is invalid.
translation_clean: The tablet of the debt of Anah-ilī [that may turn up] is invalid.

translation: If ever Anah-ilī should raise a claim against Aššur-mālik, [Kuliya and] Šu-Bēlum, they will bring the valif record of Mannum-mu.... and then this tablet will be cancelled.
translation_clean: If ever Anah-ilī should raise a claim against Aššur-mālik, [Kuliya and] Šu-Bēlum, they will bring the valif record of Mannum-mu.... and then this tablet will be cancelled.

translation: In the presence of Ištar-tūlid, son of Adaya, of Budaya, son of Tarawa[zi].
translation_clean: In the presence of Ištar-tūlid, son of Adaya, of Budaya, son of Tarawa[zi].

translation: To Kuliya, Aššur-lamassī and Aššur-lamassī, thus says Aššur-emūqī, to Aššur-lamassī, son of Puzur-ilī in particular:
translation_clean: To Kuliya, Aššur-lamassī and Aššur-lamassī, thus says Aššur-emūqī, to Aššur-lamassī, son of Puzur-ilī in particular:

translation: You have 3 shekels of silver, given by me, since the eponymy of Aššur-mālik, son of šu-Haniš.
translation_clean: You have 3 shekels of silver, given by me, since the eponymy of Aššur-mālik, son of šu-Haniš.

translation: You said: I will bring up for you from the City 3 Akkadian garments for children.
translation_clean: You said: I will bring up for you from the City 3 Akkadian garments for children.

translation: You brought these textiles up, but did not enter here with me, you went away on a journey.
translation_clean: You brought these textiles up, but did not enter here with me, you went away on a journey.

translation: Please my brother, my lord, give these three garments for children that you brought up (from Assur) to Kuliya.
translation_clean: Please my brother, my lord, give these three garments for children that you brought up (from Assur) to Kuliya.

translation: If these garments have already been sold establish how much silver they yielded and give it to Kuliya.
translation_clean: If these garments have already been sold establish how much silver they yielded and give it to Kuliya.

translation: (Then) my dear brother, I will pray for you before Aššur.
translation_clean: (Then) my dear brother, I will pray for you before Aššur.

translation: Say to my son Aššur-lamassī: Here you turned to me saying: Give me a little silver for my expenses and I will send you the merchandise it yields.
translation_clean: Say to my son Aššur-lamassī: Here you turned to me saying: Give me a little silver for my expenses and I will send you the merchandise it yields.

translation: I did give you silver and also wool and saddleclothes for a value of 8 shekels of silver, saying: I owe a cauldron to the man of Mamma, therefore send me a cauldron or a slave.
translation_clean: I did give you silver and also wool and saddleclothes for a value of 8 shekels of silver, saying: I owe a cauldron to the man of Mamma, therefore send me a cauldron or a slave.

translation: It is now two years that you manage my silver.
translation_clean: It is now two years that you manage my silver.

translation: Be so kind as to give to Kuliya that silver or what has been acquired for it during its journey.
translation_clean: Be so kind as to give to Kuliya that silver or what has been acquired for it during its journey.

translation: Seal of Aššur-taklāku, son of Ikūnum, seal of Kīnam-Aššur, son of Babālum, seal of Aššur-nada, son of Happuala.
translation_clean: Seal of Aššur-taklāku, son of Ikūnum, seal of Kīnam-Aššur, son of Babālum, seal of Aššur-nada, son of Happuala.

translation: 10 shekels of 'dust silver' Aššur-nādā owes to the creditor.
translation_clean: 10 shekels of 'dust silver' Aššur-nādā owes to the creditor.

translation: Month VI, eponymy of the successor of Abu-šalim, son of Ilī-alum.
translation_clean: Month VI, eponymy of the successor of Abu-šalim, son of Ilī-alum.

translation: He will pay within 6 months, if he does not pay he will add 1 1/2 shekel of silver per month as interest per mina.
translation_clean: He will pay within 6 months, if he does not pay he will add 1 1/2 shekel of silver per month as interest per mina.

translation: The bearer of this tablet is the creditor.
translation_clean: The bearer of this tablet is the creditor.

translation: Over there Tūrī-Ištar, son of Zuma... (is) the litigant, ask him.
translation_clean: Over there Tūrī-Ištar, son of Zuma... (is) the litigant, ask him.

translation: Thus kārum Kanesh, say to the dātu-payers, our messenger, every single colony, and the trading stations:
translation_clean: Thus kārum Kanesh, say to the dātu-payers, our messenger, every single colony, and the trading stations:

translation: A letter of the City has arrived.
translation_clean: A letter of the City has arrived.

translation: In the letter of the City (it is written):
translation_clean: In the letter of the City (it is written):

translation: From this day on, whoever buys meteoric iron, (the City of) Assur is not entitled to part of the profit made, the tithe on it kārum Kanesh will collect.
translation_clean: From this day on, whoever buys meteoric iron, (the City of) Assur is not entitled to part of the profit made, the tithe on it kārum Kanesh will collect.

translation: As soon as you have heard our letter, who(ever) over there has either sold it to a palace, or has offered it to palace officials, or still carries it with him without having yet sold it - all meteoric iron he carries, write the exact amount of every (piece of ) meteoric iron, his name and the name of his father in a tablet and send it here with our messenger.
translation_clean: As soon as you have heard our letter, who(ever) over there has either sold it to a palace, or has offered it to palace officials, or still carries it with him without having yet sold it - all meteoric iron he carries, write the exact amount of every (piece of ) meteoric iron, his name and the name of his father in a tablet and send it here with our messenger.

translation: Send a copy of (this) letter of ours to every single colony and to all the trading stations.
translation_clean: Send a copy of (this) letter of ours to every single colony and to all the trading stations.

translation: Even when somebody has sold meteoric iron via a trading agent, register the name of that man.
translation_clean: Even when somebody has sold meteoric iron via a trading agent, register the name of that man.

translation: To Aššur-ṭāb, thus says Kuliya:
translation_clean: To Aššur-ṭāb, thus says Kuliya:

translation: In accordance with your instructions we, I myself, Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, and Šū-Bēlum, son of Puqidi, have settled the affair of Anah-ilī, son of Tāb-şilli-Aššur, for 1 mina of silver and I myself guaranteed 1 mina of silver in Anah-ilī's name.
translation_clean: In accordance with your instructions we, I myself, Aššur-mālik, son of Kurara, and Šū-Bēlum, son of Puqidi, have settled the affair of Anah-ilī, son of Tāb-şilli-Aššur, for 1 mina of silver and I myself guaranteed 1 mina of silver in Anah-ilī's name.

translation: You gave me a tablet with your seal stating that you would not come back on Anah-ilī for the 1 1/3 mina of silver of his debt-note.
translation_clean: You gave me a tablet with your seal stating that you would not come back on Anah-ilī for the 1 1/3 mina of silver of his debt-note.

translation: Should Anah-ilī not receive from me the tablet with your seal, then I will send to the City, to your father Abu-šalim, 1 mina of silver and as soon as he receives that silver Abu-šalim will give to my and Anah-ilī's representatives a tablet with his seal stating that he will not come back on Anah-ilī for the 1 1/3 mina of silver recorded in his validated tablet.
translation_clean: Should Anah-ilī not receive from me the tablet with your seal, then I will send to the City, to your father Abu-šalim, 1 mina of silver and as soon as he receives that silver Abu-šalim will give to my and Anah-ilī's representatives a tablet with his seal stating that he will not come back on Anah-ilī for the 1 1/3 mina of silver recorded in his validated tablet.

translation: And then I will give you back the tablet with your seal and this letter will be cancelled.
translation_clean: And then I will give you back the tablet with your seal and this letter will be cancelled.

translation: The house in Kanesh, the house of Hinnaya, where Ilī-bāni lives I lies buried, should that house become Kuliya's property, then for the 24 shekels of silver that Ennam-Aššur paid as price for this house to Puzur-Aššur, Ennum-Aššur will produce witnesses against Kuliya and Kuliya will pay to Ennum-Aššur 1/2 mina of silver.
translation_clean: The house in Kanesh, the house of Hinnaya, where Ilī-bāni lives I lies buried, should that house become Kuliya's property, then for the 24 shekels of silver that Ennam-Aššur paid as price for this house to Puzur-Aššur, Ennum-Aššur will produce witnesses against Kuliya and Kuliya will pay to Ennum-Aššur 1/2 mina of silver.

translation: Should Kuliya not acquire that house, then Ennum-Aššur will sue the resident of that house for his silver.
translation_clean: Should Kuliya not acquire that house, then Ennum-Aššur will sue the resident of that house for his silver.

translation: In the presence of Aššur-taklāku, son of Karwaya, of Urad-Aššur, son of Watrum.
translation_clean: In the presence of Aššur-taklāku, son of Karwaya, of Urad-Aššur, son of Watrum.

translation: Thus kārum Kanesh, say to <every single> colony and the trading stations until Tuhpiya as follows:
translation_clean: Thus kārum Kanesh, say to every single colony and the trading stations until Tuhpiya as follows:

translation: Kuliya, our messenger, has been sent to transmit instructions.
translation_clean: Kuliya, our messenger, has been sent to transmit instructions.

translation: Give him two escorts and (thus) (each) colony must convey him to the (next) colony.
translation_clean: Give him two escorts and (thus) (each) colony must convey him to the (next) colony.

translation: Do not cause him delay!
translation_clean: Do not cause him delay!

translation: If among the escorts you have chosen somebody refuses to accompany Kuliya, bind him and have him brought here.
translation_clean: If among the escorts you have chosen somebody refuses to accompany Kuliya, bind him and have him brought here.

translation: 2 donkeys, for 33 shekels of silver, 2 donkeys for 25 1/2 shekels of silver, 1 1/2 shekel of silver from the price of a textle, when he bought a tisābu-garment for his wife - in all 1 mina of silver due from alutani Huanālum, witnessed by Hunadaduʾa, his son-in-law and the ..... of Alutani, and by Bazazaya.
translation_clean: 2 donkeys, for 33 shekels of silver, 2 donkeys for 25 1/2 shekels of silver, 1 1/2 shekel of silver from the price of a textle, when he bought a tisābu-garment for his wife - in all 1 mina of silver due from alutani Huanālum, witnessed by Hunadaduʾa, his son-in-law and the ..... of Alutani, and by Bazazaya.

translation: 45 shekels of silver, the price of 2 donkeys Ašdunani Hušanālum owes, witnessed by Hunadadu, by Hurdunani, by Bazazaya.
translation_clean: 45 shekels of silver, the price of 2 donkeys Ašdunani Hušanālum owes, witnessed by Hunadadu, by Hurdunani, by Bazazaya.

translation: 1/2 mina of silver, the price of 2 donkeys owed by Nanalī and Ubarzaya, his brother, witnessed by Ušubuhamazā, by Dumeni, by Hunadaduʾa, by Bazazaya.
translation_clean: 1/2 mina of silver, the price of 2 donkeys owed by Nanalī and Ubarzaya, his brother, witnessed by Ušubuhamazā, by Dumeni, by Hunadaduʾa, by Bazazaya.

translation: 24 shekels of silver the price of 2 donkeys, owed by Kuliya, the retailer, witnessed by Bazazaya, by Hunadaduam, by Uʾanamea, his partner, and by Hašutayalī.
translation_clean: 24 shekels of silver the price of 2 donkeys, owed by Kuliya, the retailer, witnessed by Bazazaya, by Hunadaduam, by Uʾanamea, his partner, and by Hašutayalī.

translation: 1/2 mina of silver, the price of 2 donkeys, owed by Bazazaya, witnessed by Urdum, by Aliya.
translation_clean: 1/2 mina of silver, the price of 2 donkeys, owed by Bazazaya, witnessed by Urdum, by Aliya.

translation: All together: 3 minas and 9 shekels, your silver, they will pay in spring.
translation_clean: All together: 3 minas and 9 shekels, your silver, they will pay in spring.

translation: Thus [Amur]-Ištar, to Kuliya:
translation_clean: Thus [Amur]-Ištar, to Kuliya:

translation: Several times I wrote you concerning my retailers Amurrum-bāni because of the price of my textiles, but you have never sent my any information!
translation_clean: Several times I wrote you concerning my retailers Amurrum-bāni because of the price of my textiles, but you have never sent my any information!

translation: I here have concern for your interests, but you have never, not even for a single day, sacrificed your own interests to care for mine!
translation_clean: I here have concern for your interests, but you have never, not even for a single day, sacrificed your own interests to care for mine!

translation: [........] silver, the price paid for [my textiles], make both Aššur-ennam [......... Šali]m-Aššur [......] and Amurrum-bāni pay that silver and send Amurrum-bāni to me.
translation_clean: [........] silver, the price paid for [my textiles], make both Aššur-ennam [......... Šali]m-Aššur [......] and Amurrum-bāni pay that silver and send Amurrum-bāni to me.

translation: Send me a message if they try to rob me of my silver and keep me informed.
translation_clean: Send me a message if they try to rob me of my silver and keep me informed.

translation: Aššur-bāni, son of Iddin-abum, owes me 1 mina of silver, make him pay and send it to me.
translation_clean: Aššur-bāni, son of Iddin-abum, owes me 1 mina of silver, make him pay and send it to me.

translation: One mina of silver is owed by Aššur-ennam, son of ........, whatever is owed to me by him, by Šamaš-annam, the servant of Aššur-ennam, and what is owed by Šalim-Aššur, make them pay!
translation_clean: One mina of silver is owed by Aššur-ennam, son of ........, whatever is owed to me by him, by Šamaš-annam, the servant of Aššur-ennam, and what is owed by Šalim-Aššur, make them pay!

translation: 10 minas of refined silver, on which a claim rests, is owed by Asānum, his servant, to Kuliya son of Ali-abum.
translation_clean: 10 minas of refined silver, on which a claim rests, is owed by Asānum, his servant, to Kuliya son of Ali-abum.

translation: He will pay the silver in one year.
translation_clean: He will pay the silver in one year.

translation: Month VIII, eponymy of Ennam-Suen, son of Iddin-abum.
translation_clean: Month VIII, eponymy of Ennam-Suen, son of Iddin-abum.

translation: For the costs of upbringing, the food and the tomb of the daughters of Kapsiya, for the tin, the venture credit of the daughter of Hanana, Hanana's wife and sons will not raise claims against Kapsiya for anything they have given to Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum.
translation_clean: For the costs of upbringing, the food and the tomb of the daughters of Kapsiya, for the tin, the venture credit of the daughter of Hanana, Hanana's wife and sons will not raise claims against Kapsiya for anything they have given to Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum.

translation: And Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum, for a silver debt of 1 mina and 7 shekels owed by Pilah-Ištar, son of Aššur-rē'ī, for 22 1/2 shekels of silver of the Anatolian of.........., and for the debt owed by Harimtum - Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum will not raise claims against Hudida and the sons of Hanana.
translation_clean: And Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum, for a silver debt of 1 mina and 7 shekels owed by Pilah-Ištar, son of Aššur-rē'ī, for 22 1/2 shekels of silver of the Anatolian of.........., and for the debt owed by Harimtum - Kapsiya, wife of Ali-abum will not raise claims against Hudida and the sons of Hanana.

translation: If, apart from the 15 shekels, which Nakile'ed has to confirm, Hanana <proves to have> an additional claim in silver on Nakile'ed, Kapsiya will have a claim on her (Hudida) for the (figurines of the) gods.
translation_clean: If, apart from the 15 shekels, which Nakile'ed has to confirm, Hanana proves to have an additional claim in silver on Nakile'ed, Kapsiya will have a claim on her (Hudida) for the (figurines of the) gods.

translation: In the presence of Aššur-lamassī, son of Šu-Kūbum, of Ennānum, of Ubaya, of Šumi-abiya, and of Amur-Šamaš.
translation_clean: In the presence of Aššur-lamassī, son of Šu-Kūbum, of Ennānum, of Ubaya, of Šumi-abiya, and of Amur-Šamaš.

translation: To Kuliya, thus says Hunahuna:
translation_clean: To Kuliya, thus says Hunahuna:

translation: Anah-ilī and Puzur-Aššur came here.
translation_clean: Anah-ilī and Puzur-Aššur came here.

translation: As for the silver, the 1 1/3 mina of silver for which Anah-ilī had become indebted, over there Puzur-Aššur consumed (for) 14 shekels of silver, [ (the rest) 1 mina 6 shekels has been entrusted to somebody else.
translation_clean: As for the silver, the 1 1/3 mina of silver for which Anah-ilī had become indebted, over there Puzur-Aššur consumed (for) 14 shekels of silver, [ (the rest) 1 mina 6 shekels has been entrusted to somebody else.

translation: Anah-ilī said: Bring me the tablet with my seal then take the silver.
translation_clean: Anah-ilī said: Bring me the tablet with my seal then take the silver.

translation: But I refused [to release that tablet.
translation_clean: But I refused [to release that tablet.

translation: Ennānātum said: We will not file a complaint lest Innaya's son hears about it.
translation_clean: Ennānātum said: We will not file a complaint lest Innaya's son hears about it.

translation: Since we became afraid, Ennānātum and I myself, we opened the storeroom and took out of your tablet coffer the tablet with his seal, which we gave him.
translation_clean: Since we became afraid, Ennānātum and I myself, we opened the storeroom and took out of your tablet coffer the tablet with his seal, which we gave him.

translation: The 1 mina and 6 shekels of silver I have in my possession.
translation_clean: The 1 mina and 6 shekels of silver I have in my possession.

translation: Since he did not give me your two letters I also did not release to him the tablet of Aššur-ṭāb.
translation_clean: Since he did not give me your two letters I also did not release to him the tablet of Aššur-ṭāb.

translation: Not one of your representatives came into action.
translation_clean: Not one of your representatives came into action.

translation: When the servants had come here, they were looking for the silver of Ennānātum , but he had absolutely nothing available (and) he was unable to give them even one single shekel of silver.
translation_clean: When the servants had come here, they were looking for the silver of Ennānātum , but he had absolutely nothing available (and) he was unable to give them even one single shekel of silver.

translation: It is now up to you.
translation_clean: It is now up to you.

translation: I myself obtained 4 shekels of silver (and) I (also) took 2 1/3 shekels of Samaya.
translation_clean: I myself obtained 4 shekels of silver (and) I (also) took 2 1/3 shekels of Samaya.

translation: I myself will use that silver for buying salt and then they can make themselves ready and depart.
translation_clean: I myself will use that silver for buying salt and then they can make themselves ready and depart.

translation: As for the girl, I recovered, I am now well.
translation_clean: As for the girl, I recovered, I am now well.

translation: Say to Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:
translation_clean: Say to Kuliya, thus says Ababaya:

translation: Exactly as your previous letter stated I have given 3 minas of silver to Ennānātum.
translation_clean: Exactly as your previous letter stated I have given 3 minas of silver to Ennānātum.

translation: Please, what is this, that you released silver to Ennānāt-Aššur?
translation_clean: Please, what is this, that you released silver to Ennānāt-Aššur?

translation: You put trust in his outward appearance, but he has neither house nor child and still you released the silver!
translation_clean: You put trust in his outward appearance, but he has neither house nor child and still you released the silver!

translation: Please, to an independent trader
translation_clean: Please, to an independent trader

translation: ..............as for my misery / expenses, ask Būr-Ištar (about it).
translation_clean: ..............as for my misery / expenses, ask Būr-Ištar (about it).

translation: All I gave him for Aššur-ṣululī has been registered on a wax tablet.
translation_clean: All I gave him for Aššur-ṣululī has been registered on a wax tablet.

translation: Please do no act highandedly.
translation_clean: Please do no act highandedly.

translation: As for the tablet of/in the house of Bēlum-bāni - when your letter comes we will obtain its release.
translation_clean: As for the tablet of/in the house of Bēlum-bāni - when your letter comes we will obtain its release.

translation: Your sister talks a lot to your father and she tries to make him forget you!
translation_clean: Your sister talks a lot to your father and she tries to make him forget you!

translation: Do not act highhandedly, you have nothing to fear since your father has nobody but you.
translation_clean: Do not act highhandedly, you have nothing to fear since your father has nobody but you.

translation: Please, the rings of meteoric iron
translation_clean: Please, the rings of meteoric iron

translation: [ ] and the....,
translation_clean: [ ] and the....,

translation: let Aššur-ṣululī [take them along] to bring (them) to me.
translation_clean: let Aššur-ṣululī [take them along] to bring (them) to me.

translation: Atata seized us against Ennam-Suen, son of Šu-Anum and Atata said:
translation_clean: Atata seized us against Ennam-Suen, son of Šu-Anum and Atata said:

translation: Look here, 3000 (minas of) copper the ruler of Wahšušana has promised to pay to you as a final settlement.
translation_clean: Look here, 3000 (minas of) copper the ruler of Wahšušana has promised to pay to you as a final settlement.

translation: You would see to it that the silver paid for it would reach me in the City.
translation_clean: You would see to it that the silver paid for it would reach me in the City.

translation: You would enjoy the profit on it and I would obtain the capital sum.
translation_clean: You would enjoy the profit on it and I would obtain the capital sum.

translation: Ennam-Suen answered: I would see to it that the silver paid for the copper reaches you in the City and I would make purchases (with it) and the profit (on it) I would enjoy.
translation_clean: Ennam-Suen answered: I would see to it that the silver paid for the copper reaches you in the City and I would make purchases (with it) and the profit (on it) I would enjoy.

translation: In the presence of Nab-Suen, son of Pilah-Ištar, of Malik-ennam, the scribe.
translation_clean: In the presence of Nab-Suen, son of Pilah-Ištar, of Malik-ennam, the scribe.

translation: 2 1/2 minas of ....... including those of Abī-wēdum.
translation_clean: 2 1/2 minas of ....... including those of Abī-wēdum.

translation: 1 1/3 mina for ......
translation_clean: 1 1/3 mina for ......

translation: For 1 mina ...... ziganšarrū, 6 minas alulītum worth 3 1/2 shekels of silver, for 10 shekels of silver tin, 8 1/2 shekels of silver for a donkey, 4 1/2 minas of copper for their head-tax, 1 mina for cowry shells.
translation_clean: For 1 mina ...... ziganšarrū, 6 minas alulītum worth 3 1/2 shekels of silver, for 10 shekels of silver tin, 8 1/2 shekels of silver for a donkey, 4 1/2 minas of copper for their head-tax, 1 mina for cowry shells.

translation: All this I gave to Aššur-ṣululī, he is satisfied.
translation_clean: All this I gave to Aššur-ṣululī, he is satisfied.

translation: To Kuliya, say, thus says Asānum:
translation_clean: To Kuliya, say, thus says Asānum:

translation: I am still staying (here) because of the meteoric iron.
translation_clean: I am still staying (here) because of the meteoric iron.

translation: When they agree with the (quality/price of the) iron I will sell it, get ready and leave for you.
translation_clean: When they agree with the (quality/price of the) iron I will sell it, get ready and leave for you.

translation: As for your letter which arrived here concerning the axe, I have not yet recorded (the depositions) of the witnesses.
translation_clean: As for your letter which arrived here concerning the axe, I have not yet recorded (the depositions) of the witnesses.

translation: Among my witnesses figures Azu, the transporter of Puzur-Aššur.
translation_clean: Among my witnesses figures Azu, the transporter of Puzur-Aššur.

translation: Please, my lord, approach Puzur Aššur and let him send Azu to me, so that he can testify for me together with his colleagues.
translation_clean: Please, my lord, approach Puzur Aššur and let him send Azu to me, so that he can testify for me together with his colleagues.

translation: Without my knowledge he has left for there saying: I am going to Elālī.
translation_clean: Without my knowledge he has left for there saying: I am going to Elālī.

translation: Threes minas of wool when I gave 1 mina of wool to the native of 'Birds-Town'.
translation_clean: Threes minas of wool when I gave 1 mina of wool to the native of 'Birds-Town'.

translation: 5 minas of wool which I had brought from Tegarama.
translation_clean: 5 minas of wool which I had brought from Tegarama.

translation: 5 minas of wool of the kārum-office.
translation_clean: 5 minas of wool of the kārum-office.

translation: 1 1/2 mina of wool, a gift which I offered to Suen.
translation_clean: 1 1/2 mina of wool, a gift which I offered to Suen.

translation: 2 1/2 minas of wool a gift which I offered to Anna.
translation_clean: 2 1/2 minas of wool a gift which I offered to Anna.

translation: 9 minas of wool from what Ennum-Aššur had given me.
translation_clean: 9 minas of wool from what Ennum-Aššur had given me.

translation: In all 21 minas I left to the maid.
translation_clean: In all 21 minas I left to the maid.

translation: 19 shekels of 'dust silver', the price of 1 mina and 48 shekels of tin, which Kannūtum has as claim on Amur-Ištar, son of Idaya - within one month he will pay.
translation_clean: 19 shekels of 'dust silver', the price of 1 mina and 48 shekels of tin, which Kannūtum has as claim on Amur-Ištar, son of Idaya - within one month he will pay.

translation: Month VIII
translation_clean: Month VIII

translation: In the presence of Šalim-Adad, the musician, of Iddin-Suen, the son of Atanaḫ.
translation_clean: In the presence of Šalim-Adad, the musician, of Iddin-Suen, the son of Atanaḫ.

translation: 17 shekels of silver, the price of a donkey, 30 minas of copper of Ištar-pilah, 4 sets of thick saddleclothes, 9 minas of refned copper from the price of a slave, 1 mina of šikku-copper with the son of Ištar-bāni, 2 jars of cumin - all this is available in Durhumit.
translation_clean: 17 shekels of silver, the price of a donkey, 30 minas of copper of Ištar-pilah, 4 sets of thick saddleclothes, 9 minas of refned copper from the price of a slave, 1 mina of šikku-copper with the son of Ištar-bāni, 2 jars of cumin - all this is available in Durhumit.

translation: 10 pirikannu-textiles, 1 talent of wool, 2 saddle-rugs, 80 pomegranates, 10 minas of olive oil, a saptinnu - and a nibrāru-textile, 2 scarfs from Zalpa, 2 donkeys with their harness - all this I gave to Aššur-imittī.
translation_clean: 10 pirikannu-textiles, 1 talent of wool, 2 saddle-rugs, 80 pomegranates, 10 minas of olive oil, a saptinnu - and a nibrāru-textile, 2 scarfs from Zalpa, 2 donkeys with their harness - all this I gave to Aššur-imittī.

translation: 1 ...... saddlecloth, 4 ropes, 2 pack-saddles, a sack, a box with dulbātum, a box with karāmu-fishm two ropes in Durhumit.
translation_clean: 1 ...... saddlecloth, 4 ropes, 2 pack-saddles, a sack, a box with dulbātum, a box with karāmu-fishm two ropes in Durhumit.

translation: A box of Ilī-bāni, son of Mutālum, 3 shekels of silver he owes.
translation_clean: A box of Ilī-bāni, son of Mutālum, 3 shekels of silver he owes.

translation: 1 1/3 mina of silver of Anah-ilī will go to Al[pilī], 1/2 mina of silver will go with the first opportunity I have.
translation_clean: 1 1/3 mina of silver of Anah-ilī will go to Al[pilī], 1/2 mina of silver will go with the first opportunity I have.

translation: All this is with Alpilī 10 minas of copper with the wife of Aššur-damiq of Tuhpiya, a donkey with its harness (and) separately 3 shekels of silver he owes to Asānum.
translation_clean: All this is with Alpilī 10 minas of copper with the wife of Aššur-damiq of Tuhpiya, a donkey with its harness (and) separately 3 shekels of silver he owes to Asānum.

translation: 9 minas of refined copper due from Urdum, who serves kārum Durhumit.
translation_clean: 9 minas of refined copper due from Urdum, who serves kārum Durhumit.

translation: 1 mina of šikku-copper due from Aššur-idī, son of Ištar-bāni.
translation_clean: 1 mina of šikku-copper due from Aššur-idī, son of Ištar-bāni.

translation: Two jars of cumin, with his seal, 13 ...... saddelclothes - all this belongs to Kuliya.
translation_clean: Two jars of cumin, with his seal, 13 ...... saddelclothes - all this belongs to Kuliya.

translation: Thus Iddin-Suen to Kuliya:
translation_clean: Thus Iddin-Suen to Kuliya:

translation: The one mina of oil of Tuhpiya and the 5 minas of copper of local origin you must not leave behind for me when you leave!
translation_clean: The one mina of oil of Tuhpiya and the 5 minas of copper of local origin you must not leave behind for me when you leave!

translation: Please, my brother, take care not to leave the oil and the copper behind for me!
translation_clean: Please, my brother, take care not to leave the oil and the copper behind for me!

translation: 21 large beams, 23 of smaller size; 6 large wooden boards, 8 of smaller size.
translation_clean: 21 large beams, 23 of smaller size; 6 large wooden boards, 8 of smaller size.

translation: 2 ...... saddleclothes which I acquried from Dudū for one jar and a ...... of salt.
translation_clean: 2 ...... saddleclothes which I acquried from Dudū for one jar and a ...... of salt.

translation_clean: And one emāsum with a capacity of ten quarts is with Ennum-Aššur, the son of the messenger, in Tegarama.

translation: Half a shekel of silver of Šamaš-bāni is due from Asānum, when he comes back he will give him 1 shekel of silver.
translation_clean: Half a shekel of silver of Šamaš-bāni is due from Asānum, when he comes back he will give him 1 shekel of silver.

translation: To Kuliya, thus says Alpilī and Aššur-lamassī:
translation_clean: To Kuliya, thus says Alpilī and Aššur-lamassī:

translation: We are well, we are staying in Tawiniya.
translation_clean: We are well, we are staying in Tawiniya.

translation: We hear rumors that the residents of Wahšušana have left the city.
translation_clean: We hear rumors that the residents of Wahšušana have left the city.

translation: Within two days, when we hear more details, we will inform you by letter where we are heading.
translation_clean: Within two days, when we hear more details, we will inform you by letter where we are heading.

translation: As for the coffers under your control, Alpilī will make enquiries.
translation_clean: As for the coffers under your control, Alpilī will make enquiries.
